name: Software Delivery Methods and Agile — additive questions
description: Question expansion derived from the attached source files; reuses existing repository topic IDs and defines only missing concepts.

questions.title: Applying Agile values and empirical delivery in practice
questions.prompt: A team releases a thin slice, measures usage, and adjusts the next slice instead of completing a year-long specification. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Defines agility by learning
expectedPoints.description: Connects agility to short feedback loops and adaptation.

expectedPoints.label: Requires working increments
expectedPoints.description: Emphasizes usable delivery rather than ceremony completion.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids false dichotomies
expectedPoints.description: Rejects no-planning and no-documentation interpretations.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Agile values and empirical delivery
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Equating Agile with no planning or no documentation." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Defines agility by learning
expectedPoints.description: Connects agility to short feedback loops and adaptation.

expectedPoints.label: Requires working increments
expectedPoints.description: Emphasizes usable delivery rather than ceremony completion.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids false dichotomies
expectedPoints.description: Rejects no-planning and no-documentation interpretations.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Calling fixed-scope mini-waterfalls sprints without real inspection and adaptation?

questions.title: Applying Scrum accountabilities, events, and artifacts in practice
questions.prompt: The Product Owner orders work toward a Product Goal; Developers select a Sprint Goal and create a Done Increment. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains accountabilities
expectedPoints.description: Separates Product Owner, Scrum Master, and Developers correctly.

expectedPoints.label: Explains artifacts and goals
expectedPoints.description: Connects backlogs and Increment to Product Goal, Sprint Goal, and Done.

expectedPoints.label: Explains event purpose
expectedPoints.description: Describes inspection and adaptation rather than status reporting.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Scrum accountabilities, events, and artifacts
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Treating the Scrum Master as a project manager assigning tasks." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains accountabilities
expectedPoints.description: Separates Product Owner, Scrum Master, and Developers correctly.

expectedPoints.label: Explains artifacts and goals
expectedPoints.description: Connects backlogs and Increment to Product Goal, Sprint Goal, and Done.

expectedPoints.label: Explains event purpose
expectedPoints.description: Describes inspection and adaptation rather than status reporting.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Turning the Daily Scrum into a status report to management?

questions.title: Applying User Story Refinement and Estimation in practice
questions.prompt: A checkout story is split by one payment path and has examples for success, decline, and retry rather than a vague complete-payments scope. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a good story
expectedPoints.description: Connects actor, need, value, and conversation.

expectedPoints.label: Uses testable criteria and slicing
expectedPoints.description: Describes observable acceptance criteria and vertical slices.

expectedPoints.label: Uses estimates responsibly
expectedPoints.description: Treats points as relative team forecasting, not hours or performance.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about User Story Refinement and Estimation
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Writing implementation tasks disguised as user value." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a good story
expectedPoints.description: Connects actor, need, value, and conversation.

expectedPoints.label: Uses testable criteria and slicing
expectedPoints.description: Describes observable acceptance criteria and vertical slices.

expectedPoints.label: Uses estimates responsibly
expectedPoints.description: Treats points as relative team forecasting, not hours or performance.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Comparing story points across teams or converting them directly to hours?

questions.title: Applying Kanban flow and work-in-progress limits in practice
questions.prompt: When testing reaches its WIP limit, developers help unblock or test existing items before pulling another feature. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains WIP limits
expectedPoints.description: Connects limited parallel work to finishing and reduced queues.

expectedPoints.label: Explains pull behavior
expectedPoints.description: States that capacity triggers new work and teams swarm on bottlenecks.

expectedPoints.label: Uses flow metrics
expectedPoints.description: Mentions lead time, cycle time, throughput, or aging.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Kanban flow and work-in-progress limits
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Using a board without WIP limits or explicit policies." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains WIP limits
expectedPoints.description: Connects limited parallel work to finishing and reduced queues.

expectedPoints.label: Explains pull behavior
expectedPoints.description: States that capacity triggers new work and teams swarm on bottlenecks.

expectedPoints.label: Uses flow metrics
expectedPoints.description: Mentions lead time, cycle time, throughput, or aging.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Maximizing individual utilization even when system lead time worsens?

questions.title: Applying XP and Test-Driven Development in practice
questions.prompt: A developer writes a failing behavioral test, implements the smallest passing change, then refactors under a green suite. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains TDD cycle
expectedPoints.description: Describes red, green, refactor and behavior-first feedback.

expectedPoints.label: Connects XP practices to flow
expectedPoints.description: Mentions CI, pairing, small releases, simple design, or collective ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Rejects vanity metrics
expectedPoints.description: States that coverage or test count is not the goal.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about XP and Test-Driven Development
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Writing tests after implementation and calling the sequence TDD." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains TDD cycle
expectedPoints.description: Describes red, green, refactor and behavior-first feedback.

expectedPoints.label: Connects XP practices to flow
expectedPoints.description: Mentions CI, pairing, small releases, simple design, or collective ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Rejects vanity metrics
expectedPoints.description: States that coverage or test count is not the goal.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Treating 100 percent coverage as proof of valuable or maintainable tests?

questions.title: Applying Delivery Metrics and Metric Gaming in practice
questions.prompt: A team combines lead time, deployment frequency, change failure rate, defects, and product outcomes to find a bottleneck. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a balanced metric set
expectedPoints.description: Combines flow, quality, reliability, and outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Connects metrics to decisions
expectedPoints.description: States what question or experiment each metric supports.

expectedPoints.label: Prevents gaming
expectedPoints.description: Rejects individual ranking, cross-team velocity comparison, and fixed numeric targets.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Delivery Metrics and Metric Gaming
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Using velocity as a productivity KPI or commitment." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a balanced metric set
expectedPoints.description: Combines flow, quality, reliability, and outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Connects metrics to decisions
expectedPoints.description: States what question or experiment each metric supports.

expectedPoints.label: Prevents gaming
expectedPoints.description: Rejects individual ranking, cross-team velocity comparison, and fixed numeric targets.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Optimizing utilization while ignoring queues, defects, and customer outcomes?

questions.title: Applying Waterfall and the V-model in practice
questions.prompt: A regulated device project may map requirement levels to corresponding test levels in a V-model with documented gates. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Explains predictive strengths
expectedPoints.description: Mentions stable scope, governance, traceability, or regulation.

expectedPoints.label: Explains feedback risk
expectedPoints.description: Connects late validation to expensive discovery of wrong assumptions.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes V-model
expectedPoints.description: Pairs specification levels with verification and validation levels.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Waterfall and the V-model
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Calling predictive methods always obsolete regardless of context." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains predictive strengths
expectedPoints.description: Mentions stable scope, governance, traceability, or regulation.

expectedPoints.label: Explains feedback risk
expectedPoints.description: Connects late validation to expensive discovery of wrong assumptions.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes V-model
expectedPoints.description: Pairs specification levels with verification and validation levels.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Assuming a signed specification eliminates requirement risk?

questions.title: Applying Adaptive Delivery Method Selection in practice
questions.prompt: A high-risk technical prototype may use Spiral-style risk cycles; a product team delivers increments; DevOps closes feedback through automated operations. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes iterative and incremental
expectedPoints.description: Separates revision cycles from usable value slices.

expectedPoints.label: Explains risk and flow methods
expectedPoints.description: Connects Spiral to risk and Lean to waste or queues.

expectedPoints.label: Explains DevOps broadly
expectedPoints.description: Covers ownership, automation, delivery, operations, and reliability feedback.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Adaptive Delivery Method Selection
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Using iterative and incremental as exact synonyms." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes iterative and incremental
expectedPoints.description: Separates revision cycles from usable value slices.

expectedPoints.label: Explains risk and flow methods
expectedPoints.description: Connects Spiral to risk and Lean to waste or queues.

expectedPoints.label: Explains DevOps broadly
expectedPoints.description: Covers ownership, automation, delivery, operations, and reliability feedback.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Describing DevOps as a separate operations team or only a CI/CD toolchain?

questions.title: Applying Scaling Agile across multiple teams in practice
questions.prompt: Several teams share quarterly objectives and integration policies, but each team releases independently and measures its own product outcomes. How would you apply this practice, what could go wrong, and which evidence would show improvement?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify the goal, apply the relevant mechanism, then discuss trade-offs, failure modes, and verification.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from coordination needs
expectedPoints.description: Names concrete cross-team dependencies, shared goals, risk, or funding needs.

expectedPoints.label: Uses boundaries and enabling platforms
expectedPoints.description: Reduces coordination through team ownership and explicit platform contracts.

expectedPoints.label: Controls framework overhead
expectedPoints.description: Measures delay and outcomes and removes ceremonies that do not improve flow.

followUps.prompt: Which changed requirement or constraint would reverse your decision?

questions.title: Challenging weak reasoning about Scaling Agile across multiple teams
questions.prompt: A teammate claims: "Introducing a scaled framework before fixing team boundaries and ownership." Challenge the claim. What is wrong or incomplete, and what would you decide instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identify the misconception, explain the correct model, and propose a safer practical decision.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from coordination needs
expectedPoints.description: Names concrete cross-team dependencies, shared goals, risk, or funding needs.

expectedPoints.label: Uses boundaries and enabling platforms
expectedPoints.description: Reduces coordination through team ownership and explicit platform contracts.

expectedPoints.label: Controls framework overhead
expectedPoints.description: Measures delay and outcomes and removes ceremonies that do not improve flow.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect this issue in practice: Measuring conformance to ceremonies instead of cross-team flow and outcomes?
